Alert.description:
Keeps users informed of important and sometimes time-sensitive changes.

Important Notes:
- The `intent` prop uses "information" (not "info") for the blue
  informational style
- Default intent is "neutral" - only set intent when you need a specific
  color/emphasis
Alert.name: Alert

asChild.description:
Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

Read the {@link https://optimizely-axiom.github.io/optiaxiom/components/#composition Composition guide} for more details.

bg.description: Set the element's background color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `bg.` (e.g., `bg.default`, `bg.accent`, `bg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

borderColor.description: Set the element's border color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `border.` (e.g., `border.default`, `border.accent`, `border.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

color.description: Set the element's text color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `fg.` (e.g., `fg.default`, `fg.accent`, `fg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

flex.description:
Set the element's `flex` CSS property. `1` grows to fill available
space; `auto` grows and shrinks to fit content; `initial` shrinks but
does not grow; `none` neither grows nor shrinks (use this instead of
`flex-shrink: 0`).

fontFamily.description: Set the element's font family. Only accepts predefined fontFamily tokens.

fontSize.description: Set the element's font size and line height (both properties are set together). Only accepts predefined fontSize tokens.

gridAutoRows.description:
Set the element's `grid-auto-rows` CSS property. The only value `fr`
sizes implicit rows to `minmax(0, 1fr)` (equal-height rows that can
shrink below their content).

gridColumn.description:
Set how many columns the element spans in a grid (`grid-column`). The
value is the span count, e.g. `2` spans 2 columns — NOT the column it
starts at.

gridTemplateColumns.description:
Control number of columns in a grid layout (`grid-template-columns`).
The value is the number of equal columns, e.g. `2` creates two equal
columns (`repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`) — use this instead of a raw
`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

h.description: Set the element's height. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

intent.description: Control the appearance by selecting between the different alert types.

m.description: Set the element's margin on all sides. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

maxH.description: Set the element's maximum height. Only accepts predefined maxSize tokens.

maxW.description: Set the element's maximum width. Only accepts predefined maxSize tokens.

mb.description: Set the element's bottom margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

ml.description: Set the element's left margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

mr.description: Set the element's right margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

mt.description: Set the element's top margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

mx.description: Set the element's left and right margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

my.description: Set the element's top and bottom margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

onDismiss.description: Show a close button inside the alert and invoke this callback when the close button is clicked.

p.description: Set the element's padding on all sides. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pb.description: Set the element's bottom padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pl.description: Set the element's left padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pr.description: Set the element's right padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pt.description: Set the element's top padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

px.description: Set the element's left and right padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

py.description: Set the element's top and bottom padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

rounded.description: Set the element's border radius. Only accepts predefined borderRadius tokens.

shadow.description: Set the element's box shadow. Only accepts predefined boxShadow tokens.

size.description:
Set the element's width and height. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

When width and height are the same, use `size` instead of setting both
`w` and `h` separately (e.g., prefer `size="24"` over `w="24" h="24"`).

transition.description:
Control which CSS properties should transition. Each value is a preset:
`colors` transitions color-related properties (background, border,
color, fill, stroke, text-decoration); `opacity` and `transform`
transition just that property; `all` transitions everything; `none`
disables transitions.

w.description: Set the element's width. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

z.description: Set the element's stacking order. Only accepts predefined zIndex tokens (e.g., popover, toast, tooltip) or numeric values (0, 10, 20, 30, auto).

AlertDialog.description:
Display a modal with important content that expects confirmation from the
user.

AlertDialog does NOT have a close (X) button in the header - users must
explicitly choose an action. This is intentional for confirmation dialogs to
prevent accidental dismissal of critical actions.

Use for confirmations, destructive actions, and critical decisions. Do not
use for general forms or informational content (use Dialog instead).

For async operations, use event.preventDefault() in AlertDialogAction onClick
to prevent auto-close, then manually close after completion.
AlertDialog.name: AlertDialog

defaultOpen.description: The initial open state in uncontrolled mode.

onOpenChange.description: Handler that is called when the open state changes.

open.description: The open state in controlled mode.

code.content:
import {
  AlertDialog,
  AlertDialogAction,
  AlertDialogBody,
  AlertDialogCancel,
  AlertDialogContent,
  AlertDialogFooter,
  AlertDialogHeader,
  AlertDialogTrigger,
} from "@optiaxiom/react";

export function App() {
  return (
    <AlertDialog>
      <AlertDialogTrigger>Delete comment</AlertDialogTrigger>

      <AlertDialogContent>
        <AlertDialogHeader>Delete comment?</AlertDialogHeader>

        <AlertDialogBody>
          The comment and all replies will be deleted.
        </AlertDialogBody>

        <AlertDialogFooter>
          <AlertDialogCancel />
          <AlertDialogAction>Yes, delete</AlertDialogAction>
        </AlertDialogFooter>
      </AlertDialogContent>
    </AlertDialog>
  );
}

AlertDialogAction.description: AlertDialogAction component from Axiom Design System
AlertDialogAction.name: AlertDialogAction

onCloseAutoFocus.description:
Event handler called when auto-focusing on close.
Can be prevented.

onEscapeKeyDown.description:
Event handler called when the escape key is down.
Can be prevented.

onFocusOutside.description:
Event handler called when the focus moves outside of the `DismissableLayer`.
Can be prevented.

onInteractOutside.description:
Event handler called when an interaction happens outside the `DismissableLayer`.
Specifically, when a `pointerdown` event happens outside or focus moves outside of it.
Can be prevented.

onOpenAutoFocus.description:
Event handler called when auto-focusing on open.
Can be prevented.

onPointerDownOutside.description:
Event handler called when the a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `DismissableLayer`.
Can be prevented.

size.description:
Control the size/width of the alert dialog box.

Size guidelines:
- sm (400px, default): Simple confirmations, brief messages, 1-2 actions
- md (600px): Moderate content, longer messages, 2-3 actions
- lg (800px): Detailed alerts with longer descriptions or more context

💡 Tip: When matching designs from screenshots, compare the content
density and action count against these guidelines. Most alerts work well
at 'sm' (default) and only need to increase if the design clearly shows
more content.

transition just that property; `all` transitions everything; `none`
disables transitions.

AvatarGroup.description:
Displays a group of avatars representing multiple users with automatic layout
and overflow handling.

Use this component whenever you have 2 or more Avatar components together,
even if they're not overlapping in the design. AvatarGroup handles consistent
sizing, spacing, overflow indicators (+N), and semantic grouping
automatically.

Don't manually layout multiple Avatars in a Flex - use AvatarGroup instead.

When to use:
- Multiple assignees on a task
- Team members or collaborators
- Participants in a conversation
- Any group of users
AvatarGroup.name: AvatarGroup

Box.description:
A primitive layout component that provides access to design tokens and
styling without custom CSS.

Use Box for simple styling (padding, margin, colors, borders) when you
don't need flexbox layout features. For flexbox layouts with
direction/alignment/gap, use Group instead.

Use `extractBoxProps` to separate sprinkle props from the rest when
rendering non-div elements with `asChild`.

Button.description:
Button component for triggering actions, with support for icons, addons, and
loading states.

Use this for standard action buttons. The component supports icon-only
buttons, buttons with leading/trailing icons, and custom addon content before
or after the main label.

When not to use:
- For menu and select buttons with a chevron use AngleMenuButton instead.
- For ellipsis menus use EllipsisMenuButton instead.
- For buttons that include label use LabelMenuButton instead.
- For expand/collapse triggers use Disclosure instead of Button with chevron
  icon and manual state.
- Don't use Button to build tab navigation - use Tabs instead.
Button.name: Button

code.content:
import { Button, Group } from "@optiaxiom/react";

export function App() {
  return (
    <Group flexDirection="column" gap="16">
      <Group gap="16">
        <Button size="sm">Small</Button>
        <Button size="md">Medium</Button>
        <Button size="lg">Large</Button>
      </Group>
      <Group gap="16">
        <Button appearance="primary" size="sm">
          Small
        </Button>
        <Button appearance="primary" size="md">
          Medium
        </Button>
        <Button appearance="primary" size="lg">
          Large
        </Button>
      </Group>
    </Group>
  );
}

sizes implicit rows to `minmax(0, 1fr)` (equal-height rows that can
shrink below their content).

gridColumn.description:
Set how many columns the element spans in a grid (`grid-column`). The
value is the span count, e.g. `2` spans 2 columns — NOT the column it
starts at.

gridTemplateColumns.description:
Control number of columns in a grid layout (`grid-template-columns`).
The value is the number of equal columns, e.g. `2` creates two equal
columns (`repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`) — use this instead of a raw
`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

code.content:
import {
  Box,
  Card,
  CardHeader,
  CardLink,
  Text,
  Tooltip,
} from "@optiaxiom/react";

export function App() {
  return (
    <Box maxW="xs" w="full">
      <Card>
        <CardHeader>
          <Tooltip
            auto
            content="Step by step: How to brainstorm a creative marketing campaign (+ free template)"
          >
            <CardLink href="../">
              <Text lineClamp="1">
                Step by step: How to brainstorm a creative marketing campaign (+
                free template)
              </Text>
            </CardLink>
          </Tooltip>
        </CardHeader>
      </Card>
    </Box>
  );
}

import { IconMusic, IconStar } from "@optiaxiom/icons";
import {
  Badge,
  Box,
  Button,
  Card,
  CardAction,
  CardCheckbox,
  CardHeader,
  CardPreview,
  Group,
} from "@optiaxiom/react";

export function App() {
  return (
    <Group flexDirection={["column", "row"]} gap="16">
      <Card size="224">
        <CardPreview
          addonTopLeft={
            <CardAction>
              <CardCheckbox />
            </CardAction>
          }
          bg="bg.page"
          flex="1"
        >
          <Box
            bg="bg.default"
            color="fg.success"
            p="16"
            rounded="md"
            shadow="sm"
          >
            <IconMusic size="32" />
          </Box>
        </CardPreview>
        <CardHeader>Checkbox</CardHeader>
      </Card>
      <Card size="224">
        <CardPreview
          addonBottomLeft={<Badge intent="warning">Duplicate</Badge>}
          addonTopLeft={
            <CardAction>
              <CardCheckbox />
            </CardAction>
          }
          addonTopRight={
            <CardAction>
              <Button aria-label="Add to favorites" icon={<IconStar />} />
            </CardAction>
          }
          bg="bg.page"
          flex="1"
        >
          <Box
            bg="bg.default"
            color="fg.success"
            p="16"
            rounded="md"
            shadow="sm"
          >
            <IconMusic size="32" />
          </Box>
        </CardPreview>
        <CardHeader>Checkbox and star button</CardHeader>
      </Card>
    </Group>
  );
}

import { IconMusic, IconStar } from "@optiaxiom/icons";
import {
  Badge,
  Box,
  Button,
  Card,
  CardCheckbox,
  CardHeader,
  CardPreview,
  Group,
} from "@optiaxiom/react";

export function App() {
  return (
    <Group flexDirection={["column", "row"]} gap="16">
      <Card size="224">
        <CardPreview addonTopLeft={<CardCheckbox />} bg="bg.page" flex="1">
          <Box
            bg="bg.default"
            color="fg.success"
            p="16"
            rounded="md"
            shadow="sm"
          >
            <IconMusic size="32" />
          </Box>
        </CardPreview>
        <CardHeader>Checkbox</CardHeader>
      </Card>
      <Card size="224">
        <CardPreview
          addonBottomLeft={<Badge intent="warning">Duplicate</Badge>}
          addonTopLeft={<CardCheckbox />}
          addonTopRight={
            <Button aria-label="Add to favorites" icon={<IconStar />} />
          }
          bg="bg.page"
          flex="1"
        >
          <Box
            bg="bg.default"
            color="fg.success"
            p="16"
            rounded="md"
            shadow="sm"
          >
            <IconMusic size="32" />
          </Box>
        </CardPreview>
        <CardHeader>Checkbox and star button</CardHeader>
      </Card>
    </Group>
  );
}

CardAction.name: CardAction

asChild.description:
Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

Read the {@link https://optimizely-axiom.github.io/optiaxiom/components/#composition Composition guide} for more details.

bg.description: Set the element's background color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `bg.` (e.g., `bg.default`, `bg.accent`, `bg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

borderColor.description: Set the element's border color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `border.` (e.g., `border.default`, `border.accent`, `border.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

color.description: Set the element's text color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `fg.` (e.g., `fg.default`, `fg.accent`, `fg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

flex.description:
Set the element's `flex` CSS property. `1` grows to fill available
space; `auto` grows and shrinks to fit content; `initial` shrinks but
does not grow; `none` neither grows nor shrinks (use this instead of
`flex-shrink: 0`).

fontFamily.description: Set the element's font family. Only accepts predefined fontFamily tokens.

fontSize.description: Set the element's font size and line height (both properties are set together). Only accepts predefined fontSize tokens.

gridAutoRows.description:
Set the element's `grid-auto-rows` CSS property. The only value `fr`
sizes implicit rows to `minmax(0, 1fr)` (equal-height rows that can
shrink below their content).

gridColumn.description:
Set how many columns the element spans in a grid (`grid-column`). The
value is the span count, e.g. `2` spans 2 columns — NOT the column it
starts at.

gridTemplateColumns.description:
Control number of columns in a grid layout (`grid-template-columns`).
The value is the number of equal columns, e.g. `2` creates two equal
columns (`repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`) — use this instead of a raw
`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

asChild.description:
Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

Read the {@link https://optimizely-axiom.github.io/optiaxiom/components/#composition Composition guide} for more details.

bg.description: Set the element's background color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `bg.` (e.g., `bg.default`, `bg.accent`, `bg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

borderColor.description: Set the element's border color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `border.` (e.g., `border.default`, `border.accent`, `border.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

color.description: Set the element's text color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `fg.` (e.g., `fg.default`, `fg.accent`, `fg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

cover.description: Whether to expand and fill up the whole area of the parent which has `position: relative`.

disabled.description: Whether to show disabled state and disable interactions.

external.description: Shows an external link icon and sets the correct rel/target attributes.

flex.description:
Set the element's `flex` CSS property. `1` grows to fill available
space; `auto` grows and shrinks to fit content; `initial` shrinks but
does not grow; `none` neither grows nor shrinks (use this instead of
`flex-shrink: 0`).

fontFamily.description: Set the element's font family. Only accepts predefined fontFamily tokens.

fontSize.description: Set the element's font size and line height (both properties are set together). Only accepts predefined fontSize tokens.

gridAutoRows.description:
Set the element's `grid-auto-rows` CSS property. The only value `fr`
sizes implicit rows to `minmax(0, 1fr)` (equal-height rows that can
shrink below their content).

gridColumn.description:
Set how many columns the element spans in a grid (`grid-column`). The
value is the span count, e.g. `2` spans 2 columns — NOT the column it
starts at.

gridTemplateColumns.description:
Control number of columns in a grid layout (`grid-template-columns`).
The value is the number of equal columns, e.g. `2` creates two equal
columns (`repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`) — use this instead of a raw
`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

h.description: Set the element's height. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

m.description: Set the element's margin on all sides. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

maxH.description: Set the element's maximum height. Only accepts predefined maxSize tokens.

maxW.description: Set the element's maximum width. Only accepts predefined maxSize tokens.

mb.description: Set the element's bottom margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

ml.description: Set the element's left margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

mr.description: Set the element's right margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

mt.description: Set the element's top margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

mx.description: Set the element's left and right margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

my.description: Set the element's top and bottom margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

p.description: Set the element's padding on all sides. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pb.description: Set the element's bottom padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pl.description: Set the element's left padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pr.description: Set the element's right padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pt.description: Set the element's top padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

px.description: Set the element's left and right padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

py.description: Set the element's top and bottom padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

rounded.description: Set the element's border radius. Only accepts predefined borderRadius tokens.

shadow.description: Set the element's box shadow. Only accepts predefined boxShadow tokens.

size.description:
Set the element's width and height. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

When width and height are the same, use `size` instead of setting both
`w` and `h` separately (e.g., prefer `size="24"` over `w="24" h="24"`).

transition.description:
Control which CSS properties should transition. Each value is a preset:
`colors` transitions color-related properties (background, border,
color, fill, stroke, text-decoration); `opacity` and `transform`
transition just that property; `all` transitions everything; `none`
disables transitions.

w.description: Set the element's width. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

z.description: Set the element's stacking order. Only accepts predefined zIndex tokens (e.g., popover, toast, tooltip) or numeric values (0, 10, 20, 30, auto).

shrink below their content).

value is the span count, e.g. `2` spans 2 columns — NOT the column it
starts at.

columns (`repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`) — use this instead of a raw
`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

DateInput.description:
Input field with calendar that lets user enter dates.

Important Notes:
- The `placeholder` prop sets the default date/time in the calendar picker,
  NOT hint text for the input field (native date inputs don't support that)
- Don't use `addonBefore` as it will override the calendar icon that opens
  the date picker popover
DateInput.name: DateInput

placeholder.description:
The placeholder date and time to use when no value has yet been selected.

The value format must an Instant, PlainDate, PlainDateTime, or PlainTime.
The default date and time to use in the calendar picker when no value has
been selected.

**NOTE:** This is NOT a placeholder hint text for the input field. Native
date inputs do not support placeholder text. The value format must be an
Instant, PlainDate, PlainDateTime, or PlainTime (e.g., "2024-01-01",
"2024-01-01T12:00", "12:00").
@example // Set default time to noon when picking dates
<DateInput placeholder="12:00" />

// Set default date to a specific date
<DateInput placeholder="2024-01-01" />

transition just that property; `all` transitions everything; `none`

`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

Can be prevented.

Can be prevented.

onInteractOutside.description:
Event handler called when an interaction happens outside the `DismissableLayer`.
Specifically, when a `pointerdown` event happens outside or focus moves outside of it.
Can be prevented.

onOpenAutoFocus.description:
Event handler called when auto-focusing on open.
Can be prevented.

onPointerDownOutside.description:
Event handler called when the a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `DismissableLayer`.
Can be prevented.

z.description: Set the element's stacking order. Only accepts predefined zIndex tokens (e.g., popover, toast, tooltip) or numeric values (0, 10, 20, 30, auto).

Dialog.description:
Display a modal dialog box.

DialogHeader automatically includes a close button (X) in the top-right
corner - do not manually add a close button to the header.

Use Dialog for forms, data entry, content display, multi-step workflows, and
non-critical interactions. For confirmations or destructive actions, use
AlertDialog instead (which has no close button and requires explicit user
choice).

For forms, wrap DialogBody and DialogFooter in DialogForm and use
type="submit" on the primary action button.
Dialog.name: Dialog

defaultOpen.description: The initial open state in uncontrolled mode.

onOpenChange.description: Handler that is called when the open state changes.

open.description: The open state in controlled mode.

code.content:
import {
  Dialog,
  DialogBody,
  DialogClose,
  DialogContent,
  DialogFooter,
  DialogHeader,
  DialogTrigger,
} from "@optiaxiom/react";

export function App() {
  return (
    <Dialog>
      <DialogTrigger>Open Dialog</DialogTrigger>

      <DialogContent size="sm">
        <DialogHeader>Modal Title</DialogHeader>

        <DialogBody>This is the modal body</DialogBody>

        <DialogFooter>
          <Dialog>
            <DialogTrigger mr="auto">Open nested dialog</DialogTrigger>

            <DialogContent size="sm">
              <DialogHeader>Modal Title</DialogHeader>

              <DialogBody>This is the modal body</DialogBody>

              <DialogFooter>
                <Dialog>
                  <DialogTrigger mr="auto">Open nested dialog</DialogTrigger>

                  <DialogContent size="sm">
                    <DialogHeader>Modal Title</DialogHeader>

                    <DialogBody>This is the modal body</DialogBody>

                    <DialogFooter>
                      <DialogClose appearance="primary">Close</DialogClose>
                    </DialogFooter>
                  </DialogContent>
                </Dialog>
                <DialogClose appearance="primary">Close</DialogClose>
              </DialogFooter>
            </DialogContent>
          </Dialog>
          <DialogClose appearance="primary">Close</DialogClose>
        </DialogFooter>
      </DialogContent>
    </Dialog>
  );
}

onCloseAutoFocus.description:
Event handler called when auto-focusing on close.
Can be prevented.

onEscapeKeyDown.description:
Event handler called when the escape key is down.
Can be prevented.

onFocusOutside.description:
Event handler called when the focus moves outside of the `DismissableLayer`.
Can be prevented.

onInteractOutside.description:
Event handler called when an interaction happens outside the `DismissableLayer`.
Specifically, when a `pointerdown` event happens outside or focus moves outside of it.
Can be prevented.

onOpenAutoFocus.description:
Event handler called when auto-focusing on open.
Can be prevented.

onPointerDownOutside.description:
Event handler called when the a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `DismissableLayer`.
Can be prevented.

size.description:
Control the size/width of the dialog box.

Size guidelines:
- sm (400px): Simple forms, confirmations, 1-2 input fields
- md (600-800px, default): Standard forms, 3-5 fields, medium content
- lg (900-1400px): Complex forms, wide tables, multi-column layouts
- fullscreen: Very large content, editors, dashboards

💡 Tip: When matching designs from screenshots, compare the content
density and layout complexity against these guidelines. Start with 'md'
(default) and only increase if the design clearly shows wider content or
multi-column layouts.

transition just that property; `all` transitions everything; `none`
disables transitions.

Disclosure.description:
Interactive component for expanding and collapsing content panels with
built-in state management.

Use this for collapsible sections with expand/collapse triggers. Disclosure
handles the toggle state, animations, and accessibility automatically.

When to use:
- FAQ sections
- Expandable content panels
- Show/hide details sections
- Any expand/collapse pattern with a chevron icon

Don't manually build with Button + chevron icon + useState - use Disclosure
instead.

code.content:
"use client";

import {
  DropdownMenu,
  DropdownMenuContent,
  DropdownMenuItem,
  DropdownMenuLabel,
  DropdownMenuSeparator,
  DropdownMenuSub,
  DropdownMenuSubContent,
  DropdownMenuSubTrigger,
  DropdownMenuTrigger,
} from "@optiaxiom/react";

export function App() {
  return (
    <DropdownMenu>
      <DropdownMenuTrigger>Profile</DropdownMenuTrigger>

      <DropdownMenuContent>
        <DropdownMenuLabel>My Profile</DropdownMenuLabel>
        <DropdownMenuSub>
          <DropdownMenuSubTrigger>Settings</DropdownMenuSubTrigger>
          <DropdownMenuSubContent>
            <DropdownMenuItem>Privacy</DropdownMenuItem>
            <DropdownMenuItem>Security</DropdownMenuItem>
            <DropdownMenuItem>Policy</DropdownMenuItem>
          </DropdownMenuSubContent>
        </DropdownMenuSub>
        <DropdownMenuSeparator />
        <DropdownMenuItem>Logout</DropdownMenuItem>
      </DropdownMenuContent>
    </DropdownMenu>
  );
}

asChild.description:
Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

Read the {@link https://optimizely-axiom.github.io/optiaxiom/components/#composition Composition guide} for more details.

bg.description: Set the element's background color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `bg.` (e.g., `bg.default`, `bg.accent`, `bg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

borderColor.description: Set the element's border color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `border.` (e.g., `border.default`, `border.accent`, `border.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

color.description: Set the element's text color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `fg.` (e.g., `fg.default`, `fg.accent`, `fg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

flex.description:
Set the element's `flex` CSS property. `1` grows to fill available
space; `auto` grows and shrinks to fit content; `initial` shrinks but
does not grow; `none` neither grows nor shrinks (use this instead of
`flex-shrink: 0`).

fontFamily.description: Set the element's font family. Only accepts predefined fontFamily tokens.

fontSize.description: Set the element's font size and line height (both properties are set together). Only accepts predefined fontSize tokens.

gridAutoRows.description:
Set the element's `grid-auto-rows` CSS property. The only value `fr`
sizes implicit rows to `minmax(0, 1fr)` (equal-height rows that can
shrink below their content).

gridColumn.description:
Set how many columns the element spans in a grid (`grid-column`). The
value is the span count, e.g. `2` spans 2 columns — NOT the column it
starts at.

gridTemplateColumns.description:
Control number of columns in a grid layout (`grid-template-columns`).
The value is the number of equal columns, e.g. `2` creates two equal
columns (`repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`) — use this instead of a raw
`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

px.description: Set the element's left and right padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

py.description: Set the element's top and bottom padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

rounded.description: Set the element's border radius. Only accepts predefined borderRadius tokens.

shadow.description: Set the element's box shadow. Only accepts predefined boxShadow tokens.

size.description:
Set the element's width and height. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

When width and height are the same, use `size` instead of setting both
`w` and `h` separately (e.g., prefer `size="24"` over `w="24" h="24"`).

transition.description:
Control which CSS properties should transition. Each value is a preset:
`colors` transitions color-related properties (background, border,
color, fill, stroke, text-decoration); `opacity` and `transform`
transition just that property; `all` transitions everything; `none`
disables transitions.

- When direction is 'column', alignItems defaults to 'stretch'

Group.description:
A flexbox layout component for grouping items horizontally or vertically.

Default Props:
- flexDirection: 'row' (CSS standard - horizontal layout)
- alignItems: 'center' (when row) or 'stretch' (when column)

Common Usage:
- Horizontal button groups: <Group gap="8"><Button /><Button /></Group>
- Vertical forms: <Group flexDirection="column" gap="16"><Input /><Input /></Group>
- Icon + text combos: <Group gap="4" alignItems="center"><Icon /><Text /></Group>
Group.name: Group

Heading.description:
Renders semantic heading elements (h1-h4) for document structure and
accessibility.

Use this for page titles, section headings, and any text that represents a
heading in the document outline. Don't use Text with large fontSize and bold
fontWeight for headings - Heading provides proper semantics for screen
readers and improves accessibility.

When to use:
- Page titles
- Section headings
- Card/panel titles
- Any text with fontSize='xl'+ and fontWeight='500'+

⚠️ All heading levels (1-4) default to fontWeight="700". If your design shows
lighter headings, explicitly set fontWeight="500" or "600".

Don't use Text component for headings - it lacks semantic meaning.
Heading.name: Heading

level.description:
Heading level (1-4) that controls both the semantic HTML tag and font size.
- `level="1"`: renders `<h1>` with `fontSize="4xl"` (default)
- `level="2"`: renders `<h2>` with `fontSize="3xl"`
- `level="3"`: renders `<h3>` with `fontSize="2xl"`
- `level="4"`: renders `<h4>` with `fontSize="xl"`

Use `asChild` to decouple the semantic level from visual appearance.

Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

Read the {@link https://optimizely-axiom.github.io/optiaxiom/components/#composition Composition guide} for more details.

bg.description: Set the element's background color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `bg.` (e.g., `bg.default`, `bg.accent`, `bg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

borderColor.description: Set the element's border color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `border.` (e.g., `border.default`, `border.accent`, `border.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

color.description: Set the element's text color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `fg.` (e.g., `fg.default`, `fg.accent`, `fg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

flex.description:
Set the element's `flex` CSS property. `1` grows to fill available
space; `auto` grows and shrinks to fit content; `initial` shrinks but
does not grow; `none` neither grows nor shrinks (use this instead of
`flex-shrink: 0`).

fontFamily.description: Set the element's font family. Only accepts predefined fontFamily tokens.

fontSize.description: Set the element's font size and line height (both properties are set together). Only accepts predefined fontSize tokens.

gridAutoRows.description:
Set the element's `grid-auto-rows` CSS property. The only value `fr`
sizes implicit rows to `minmax(0, 1fr)` (equal-height rows that can
shrink below their content).

gridColumn.description:
Set how many columns the element spans in a grid (`grid-column`). The
value is the span count, e.g. `2` spans 2 columns — NOT the column it
starts at.

gridTemplateColumns.description:
Control number of columns in a grid layout (`grid-template-columns`).
The value is the number of equal columns, e.g. `2` creates two equal
columns (`repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`) — use this instead of a raw
`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

h.description: Set the element's height. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

m.description: Set the element's margin on all sides. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

maxH.description: Set the element's maximum height. Only accepts predefined maxSize tokens.

maxW.description: Set the element's maximum width. Only accepts predefined maxSize tokens.

mb.description: Set the element's bottom margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

ml.description: Set the element's left margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

mr.description: Set the element's right margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

mt.description: Set the element's top margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

mx.description: Set the element's left and right margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

my.description: Set the element's top and bottom margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

onEscapeKeyDown.description:
Event handler called when the escape key is down.
Can be prevented.

onFocusOutside.description:
Event handler called when the focus moves outside of the `HoverCard`.
Can be prevented.

onInteractOutside.description:
Event handler called when an interaction happens outside the `HoverCard`.
Specifically, when a `pointerdown` event happens outside or focus moves outside of it.
Can be prevented.

onPointerDownOutside.description:
Event handler called when the a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `HoverCard`.
Can be prevented.

p.description: Set the element's padding on all sides. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pb.description: Set the element's bottom padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pl.description: Set the element's left padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pr.description: Set the element's right padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pt.description: Set the element's top padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

px.description: Set the element's left and right padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

py.description: Set the element's top and bottom padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

rounded.description: Set the element's border radius. Only accepts predefined borderRadius tokens.

shadow.description: Set the element's box shadow. Only accepts predefined boxShadow tokens.

size.description:
Set the element's width and height. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

When width and height are the same, use `size` instead of setting both
`w` and `h` separately (e.g., prefer `size="24"` over `w="24" h="24"`).

transition.description:
Control which CSS properties should transition. Each value is a preset:
`colors` transitions color-related properties (background, border,
color, fill, stroke, text-decoration); `opacity` and `transform`
transition just that property; `all` transitions everything; `none`
disables transitions.

w.description: Set the element's width. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

z.description: Set the element's stacking order. Only accepts predefined zIndex tokens (e.g., popover, toast, tooltip) or numeric values (0, 10, 20, 30, auto).

code.content:
import { Field, Group, Input } from "@optiaxiom/react";

export function App() {
  return (
    <Group flexDirection="column" gap="16">
      <Field label="Price">
        <Input addonBefore="$" placeholder="0.00" type="number" />
      </Field>
      <Field label="Discount">
        <Input addonAfter="%" placeholder="0" type="number" />
      </Field>
      <Field label="Weight">
        <Input addonAfter="kg" placeholder="0" type="number" />
      </Field>
      <Field label="Website">
        <Input addonBefore="https://" placeholder="example.com" type="url" />
      </Field>
      <Field label="Username">
        <Input addonBefore="@" placeholder="username" />
      </Field>
    </Group>
  );
}

import { Field, Group, Input } from "@optiaxiom/react";

export function App() {
  return (
    <Group flexDirection="column" gap="16">
      <Field label="Email">
        <Input placeholder="name@example.com" type="email" />
      </Field>
      <Field label="Password">
        <Input placeholder="Enter password" type="password" />
      </Field>
      <Field label="Phone number">
        <Input placeholder="+1 (555) 000-0000" type="tel" />
      </Field>
      <Field label="Website">
        <Input placeholder="https://example.com" type="url" />
      </Field>
      <Field label="Number">
        <Input placeholder="0" type="number" />
      </Field>
    </Group>
  );
}

asChild.description:
Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

Read the {@link https://optimizely-axiom.github.io/optiaxiom/components/#composition Composition guide} for more details.

bg.description: Set the element's background color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `bg.` (e.g., `bg.default`, `bg.accent`, `bg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

borderColor.description: Set the element's border color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `border.` (e.g., `border.default`, `border.accent`, `border.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

color.description: Set the element's text color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `fg.` (e.g., `fg.default`, `fg.accent`, `fg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

flex.description:
Set the element's `flex` CSS property. `1` grows to fill available
space; `auto` grows and shrinks to fit content; `initial` shrinks but
does not grow; `none` neither grows nor shrinks (use this instead of
`flex-shrink: 0`).

fontFamily.description: Set the element's font family. Only accepts predefined fontFamily tokens.

fontSize.description: Set the element's font size and line height (both properties are set together). Only accepts predefined fontSize tokens.

gridAutoRows.description:
Set the element's `grid-auto-rows` CSS property. The only value `fr`
sizes implicit rows to `minmax(0, 1fr)` (equal-height rows that can
shrink below their content).

gridColumn.description:
Set how many columns the element spans in a grid (`grid-column`). The
value is the span count, e.g. `2` spans 2 columns — NOT the column it
starts at.

gridTemplateColumns.description:
Control number of columns in a grid layout (`grid-template-columns`).
The value is the number of equal columns, e.g. `2` creates two equal
columns (`repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`) — use this instead of a raw
`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

Menu.description:
Dropdown menu for actions, multi-select, and complex interactions.
Supports filtering/search, checkbox/radio items, nested sub-menus,
links, and action handlers via `execute`.

Must be used with MenuTrigger and MenuContent sub-components. MenuTrigger
automatically renders a button with chevron icon, or use MenuTrigger asChild
with custom buttons like EllipsisMenuButton for overflow menus.

Use Menu when: action menus (edit/delete/archive), multi-select dropdowns,
filterable lists, nested menus, or items with links/navigation.

Use Select instead when: you need a simple single-select form input
with `value`/`onChange` and form submission support.

Note: Menu is not a form control - it has no `value`/`onChange` and does
not integrate with `Field`. For a single-select form field, use Select
(which does not support filtering). There is currently no built-in
filterable single-select form input.
Menu.name: Menu

defaultOpen.description: The initial open state in uncontrolled mode.

inputValue.description: The input value in controlled mode.

inputVisible.description: Whether to always show the input or only if there are selectable options and/or when user starts to type.

loading.description: Whether to show loading spinner or skeleton items inside the menu.

onInputValueChange.description: Handler that is called when input value changes.

onOpenChange.description: Handler that is called when the open state changes.

open.description: The open state in controlled mode.

options.description: The items we want to render.

code.content:
export type Color = { label: string; value: string };

export const colors = [
  { color: "#00B8D9", label: "Ocean", value: "ocean" },
  { color: "#0052CC", label: "Blue", value: "blue" },
  { color: "#5243AA", label: "Purple", value: "purple" },
  { color: "#FF5630", label: "Red", value: "red" },
  { color: "#FF8B00", label: "Orange", value: "orange" },
  { color: "#FFC400", label: "Yellow", value: "yellow" },
  { color: "#36B37E", label: "Green", value: "green" },
  { color: "#00875A", label: "Forest", value: "forest" },
  { color: "#253858", label: "Slate", value: "slate" },
  { color: "#666666", label: "Silver", value: "silver" },
];

MenuContent.description:
Menu content rendered in a portal with absolute positioning.

Rendered as a popover in larger screens and in a dialog in smaller screens by
default.
MenuContent.name: MenuContent

onCloseAutoFocus.description:
Event handler called when auto-focusing on close.
Can be prevented.

onEscapeKeyDown.description:
Event handler called when the escape key is down.
Can be prevented.

onFocusOutside.description:
Event handler called when the focus moves outside of the `DismissableLayer`.
Can be prevented.

onInteractOutside.description:
Event handler called when an interaction happens outside the `DismissableLayer`.
Specifically, when a `pointerdown` event happens outside or focus moves outside of it.
Can be prevented.

onOpenAutoFocus.description:
Event handler called when auto-focusing on open.
Can be prevented.

onPointerDownOutside.description:
Event handler called when the a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `DismissableLayer`.
Can be prevented.

Set the element's width and height. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

When width and height are the same, use `size` instead of setting both
`w` and `h` separately (e.g., prefer `size="24"` over `w="24" h="24"`).

transition.description:
Control which CSS properties should transition. Each value is a preset:
`colors` transitions color-related properties (background, border,
color, fill, stroke, text-decoration); `opacity` and `transform`
transition just that property; `all` transitions everything; `none`
disables transitions.

w.description: Set the element's width. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

z.description: Set the element's stacking order. Only accepts predefined zIndex tokens (e.g., popover, toast, tooltip) or numeric values (0, 10, 20, 30, auto).

NavGroupTrigger.name: NavGroupTrigger

addonAfter.description: Display content after the button.

addonBefore.description: Display content before the button.

asChild.description:
Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

Read the {@link https://optimizely-axiom.github.io/optiaxiom/components/#composition Composition guide} for more details.

bg.description: Set the element's background color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `bg.` (e.g., `bg.default`, `bg.accent`, `bg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

borderColor.description: Set the element's border color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `border.` (e.g., `border.default`, `border.accent`, `border.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

chevronPosition.description: Control which side to place the chevron.

color.description: Set the element's text color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `fg.` (e.g., `fg.default`, `fg.accent`, `fg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

flex.description:
Set the element's `flex` CSS property. `1` grows to fill available
space; `auto` grows and shrinks to fit content; `initial` shrinks but
does not grow; `none` neither grows nor shrinks (use this instead of
`flex-shrink: 0`).

fontFamily.description: Set the element's font family. Only accepts predefined fontFamily tokens.

fontSize.description: Set the element's font size and line height (both properties are set together). Only accepts predefined fontSize tokens.

gridAutoRows.description:
Set the element's `grid-auto-rows` CSS property. The only value `fr`
sizes implicit rows to `minmax(0, 1fr)` (equal-height rows that can
shrink below their content).

gridColumn.description:
Set how many columns the element spans in a grid (`grid-column`). The
value is the span count, e.g. `2` spans 2 columns — NOT the column it
starts at.

gridTemplateColumns.description:
Control number of columns in a grid layout (`grid-template-columns`).
The value is the number of equal columns, e.g. `2` creates two equal
columns (`repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`) — use this instead of a raw
`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

asChild.description:
Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

Read the {@link https://optimizely-axiom.github.io/optiaxiom/components/#composition Composition guide} for more details.

bg.description: Set the element's background color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `bg.` (e.g., `bg.default`, `bg.accent`, `bg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

borderColor.description: Set the element's border color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `border.` (e.g., `border.default`, `border.accent`, `border.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

color.description: Set the element's text color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `fg.` (e.g., `fg.default`, `fg.accent`, `fg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

flex.description:
Set the element's `flex` CSS property. `1` grows to fill available
space; `auto` grows and shrinks to fit content; `initial` shrinks but
does not grow; `none` neither grows nor shrinks (use this instead of
`flex-shrink: 0`).

fontFamily.description: Set the element's font family. Only accepts predefined fontFamily tokens.

fontSize.description: Set the element's font size and line height (both properties are set together). Only accepts predefined fontSize tokens.

gridAutoRows.description:
Set the element's `grid-auto-rows` CSS property. The only value `fr`
sizes implicit rows to `minmax(0, 1fr)` (equal-height rows that can
shrink below their content).

gridColumn.description:
Set how many columns the element spans in a grid (`grid-column`). The
value is the span count, e.g. `2` spans 2 columns — NOT the column it
starts at.

gridTemplateColumns.description:
Control number of columns in a grid layout (`grid-template-columns`).
The value is the number of equal columns, e.g. `2` creates two equal
columns (`repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`) — use this instead of a raw
`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

code.content:
import { IconGrid, IconRectangleList, IconSliders } from "@optiaxiom/icons";
import {
  Field,
  Popover,
  PopoverContent,
  PopoverTrigger,
  SegmentedControl,
  SegmentedControlItem,
  Select,
  SelectContent,
  SelectTrigger,
  Separator,
  Tooltip,
} from "@optiaxiom/react";

export function App() {
  return (
    <Popover>
      <PopoverTrigger icon={<IconSliders />}>Display</PopoverTrigger>

      <PopoverContent align="end" gap="12" px="0">
        <SegmentedControl defaultValue="list" gap="12" px="16">
          <Tooltip content="List">
            <SegmentedControlItem
              aria-label="List"
              icon={<IconRectangleList />}
              size="lg"
              value="list"
            />
          </Tooltip>

          <Tooltip content="Grid">
            <SegmentedControlItem
              aria-label="Grid"
              icon={<IconGrid />}
              size="lg"
              value="grid"
            />
          </Tooltip>
        </SegmentedControl>

        <Field
          flexDirection="row"
          justifyContent="space-between"
          label="Columns"
          px="16"
        >
          <Select
            defaultValue="Status"
            options={[
              { label: "Status", value: "Status" },
              { label: "Due Dates", value: "Due Dates" },
            ]}
          >
            <SelectTrigger ml="12" size="sm" />
            <SelectContent align="end" />
          </Select>
        </Field>

        <Field
          flexDirection="row"
          justifyContent="space-between"
          label="Grouping"
          px="16"
        >
          <Select
            defaultValue="No grouping"
            options={[
              { label: "No grouping", value: "No grouping" },
              { label: "Campaign", value: "Campaign" },
            ]}
          >
            <SelectTrigger ml="12" size="sm" />
            <SelectContent align="end" />
          </Select>
        </Field>

        <Separator orientation="horizontal" />

        <Field
          flexDirection="row"
          justifyContent="space-between"
          label="View"
          px="16"
        >
          <Select
            defaultValue="Tasks"
            options={[
              { label: "Tasks", value: "Tasks" },
              { label: "Steps", value: "Steps" },
            ]}
          >
            <SelectTrigger ml="12" size="sm" />
            <SelectContent align="end" />
          </Select>
        </Field>
      </PopoverContent>
    </Popover>
  );
}

flex.description:
Set the element's `flex` CSS property. `1` grows to fill available
space; `auto` grows and shrinks to fit content; `initial` shrinks but
does not grow; `none` neither grows nor shrinks (use this instead of
`flex-shrink: 0`).

gridAutoRows.description:

sizes implicit rows to `minmax(0, 1fr)` (equal-height rows that can
shrink below their content).

gridColumn.description:

value is the span count, e.g. `2` spans 2 columns — NOT the column it
starts at.

gridTemplateColumns.description:

The value is the number of equal columns, e.g. `2` creates two equal
columns (`repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`) — use this instead of a raw
`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

bg.description: Set the element's background color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `bg.` (e.g., `bg.default`, `bg.accent`, `bg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

borderColor.description: Set the element's border color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `border.` (e.g., `border.default`, `border.accent`, `border.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

defaultValue.description: The initial selected value in uncontrolled mode.

disabled.description: Whether the input is disabled and in read only mode.

flex.description:
Set the element's `flex` CSS property. `1` grows to fill available
space; `auto` grows and shrinks to fit content; `initial` shrinks but
does not grow; `none` neither grows nor shrinks (use this instead of
`flex-shrink: 0`).

fontFamily.description: Set the element's font family. Only accepts predefined fontFamily tokens.

fontSize.description: Set the element's font size and line height (both properties are set together). Only accepts predefined fontSize tokens.

gridAutoRows.description:
Set the element's `grid-auto-rows` CSS property. The only value `fr`
sizes implicit rows to `minmax(0, 1fr)` (equal-height rows that can
shrink below their content).

gridColumn.description:
Set how many columns the element spans in a grid (`grid-column`). The
value is the span count, e.g. `2` spans 2 columns — NOT the column it
starts at.

gridTemplateColumns.description:
Control number of columns in a grid layout (`grid-template-columns`).
The value is the number of equal columns, e.g. `2` creates two equal
columns (`repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`) — use this instead of a raw
`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

Select.description:
Single-select form input for choosing one value from a dropdown list.
Works like a native `<select>` — supports `name`, `value`, `onChange`,
and works with `<form>` submissions.

Must be used with SelectTrigger and SelectContent sub-components.

Use Select when: single value selection in a form, controlled/uncontrolled
form input, you need form submission support.

Use Menu instead when: you need multi-select, actions (edit/delete),
links, filtering/search, nested sub-menus, or checkbox/radio items.
Select.name: Select

onChange.description: Native `change` event handler for the hidden select element. Prefer `onValueChange` for most use cases.

onValueChange.description: Handler that is called when the selected value changes. Use this to track the selected value.

`colors` transitions color-related properties (background, border,
color, fill, stroke, text-decoration); `opacity` and `transform`
transition just that property; `all` transitions everything; `none`
disables transitions.

w.description: Set the element's width. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

z.description: Set the element's stacking order. Only accepts predefined zIndex tokens (e.g., popover, toast, tooltip) or numeric values (0, 10, 20, 30, auto).

SelectTrigger.description: SelectTrigger component from Axiom Design System
SelectTrigger.name: SelectTrigger

addonAfter.description: Display content inside the button after `children`.

addonBefore.description: Display content inside the button before `children`.

appearance.description: Control the appearance by selecting between the different button types.

asChild.description:
Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

Read the {@link https://optimizely-axiom.github.io/optiaxiom/components/#composition Composition guide} for more details.

bg.description: Set the element's background color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `bg.` (e.g., `bg.default`, `bg.accent`, `bg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

borderColor.description: Set the element's border color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `border.` (e.g., `border.default`, `border.accent`, `border.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

color.description: Set the element's text color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `fg.` (e.g., `fg.default`, `fg.accent`, `fg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

flex.description:
Set the element's `flex` CSS property. `1` grows to fill available
space; `auto` grows and shrinks to fit content; `initial` shrinks but
does not grow; `none` neither grows nor shrinks (use this instead of
`flex-shrink: 0`).

fontFamily.description: Set the element's font family. Only accepts predefined fontFamily tokens.

fontSize.description: Set the element's font size and line height (both properties are set together). Only accepts predefined fontSize tokens.

gridAutoRows.description:
Set the element's `grid-auto-rows` CSS property. The only value `fr`
sizes implicit rows to `minmax(0, 1fr)` (equal-height rows that can
shrink below their content).

gridColumn.description:
Set how many columns the element spans in a grid (`grid-column`). The
value is the span count, e.g. `2` spans 2 columns — NOT the column it
starts at.

gridTemplateColumns.description:
Control number of columns in a grid layout (`grid-template-columns`).
The value is the number of equal columns, e.g. `2` creates two equal
columns (`repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`) — use this instead of a raw
`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

h.description: Set the element's height. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

icon.description: Display an icon before or after the button content or omit `children` to only show the icon.

iconPosition.description: Control whether to show the icon before or after the button content.

loading.description: Whether to show loading spinner inside the button.

The value is the number of equal columns, e.g. `2` creates two equal
columns (`repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`) — use this instead of a raw
`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

transition.description:
Control which CSS properties should transition. Each value is a preset:
`colors` transitions color-related properties (background, border,
color, fill, stroke, text-decoration); `opacity` and `transform`
transition just that property; `all` transitions everything; `none`
disables transitions.

bg.description: Set the element's background color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `bg.` (e.g., `bg.default`, `bg.accent`, `bg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

borderColor.description: Set the element's border color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `border.` (e.g., `border.default`, `border.accent`, `border.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

color.description: Set the element's text color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `fg.` (e.g., `fg.default`, `fg.accent`, `fg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

flex.description:
Set the element's `flex` CSS property. `1` grows to fill available
space; `auto` grows and shrinks to fit content; `initial` shrinks but
does not grow; `none` neither grows nor shrinks (use this instead of
`flex-shrink: 0`).

fontFamily.description: Set the element's font family. Only accepts predefined fontFamily tokens.

fontSize.description: Set the element's font size and line height (both properties are set together). Only accepts predefined fontSize tokens.

Set the element's `grid-auto-rows` CSS property. The only value `fr`
sizes implicit rows to `minmax(0, 1fr)` (equal-height rows that can
shrink below their content).

gridColumn.description:
Set how many columns the element spans in a grid (`grid-column`). The
value is the span count, e.g. `2` spans 2 columns — NOT the column it
starts at.

Can be prevented.

Can be prevented.

onInteractOutside.description:
Event handler called when an interaction happens outside the `DismissableLayer`.
Specifically, when a `pointerdown` event happens outside or focus moves outside of it.
Can be prevented.

Can be prevented.

Can be prevented.

Table.description:
Display tabular data using rows and columns.

⚠️ **IMPORTANT**: Consider using `DataTable` instead for displaying tabular
data.

`DataTable` provides built-in support for:
- Sorting (client-side and server-side)
- Pagination (client-side and server-side)
- Row selection with checkboxes
- Column pinning (sticky columns)
- Row expansion
- Loading states
- Virtualization for large datasets

`Table` is a low-level primitive and should only be used for very specific
custom table layouts. For most use cases, `DataTable` is the recommended
choice.
Table.name: Table

asChild.description:
Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

Read the {@link https://optimizely-axiom.github.io/optiaxiom/components/#composition Composition guide} for more details.

bg.description: Set the element's background color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `bg.` (e.g., `bg.default`, `bg.accent`, `bg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

borderColor.description: Set the element's border color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `border.` (e.g., `border.default`, `border.accent`, `border.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

color.description: Set the element's text color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `fg.` (e.g., `fg.default`, `fg.accent`, `fg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

flex.description:
Set the element's `flex` CSS property. `1` grows to fill available
space; `auto` grows and shrinks to fit content; `initial` shrinks but
does not grow; `none` neither grows nor shrinks (use this instead of
`flex-shrink: 0`).

fontFamily.description: Set the element's font family. Only accepts predefined fontFamily tokens.

fontSize.description: Set the element's font size and line height (both properties are set together). Only accepts predefined fontSize tokens.

gridAutoRows.description:
Set the element's `grid-auto-rows` CSS property. The only value `fr`
sizes implicit rows to `minmax(0, 1fr)` (equal-height rows that can
shrink below their content).

gridColumn.description:
Set how many columns the element spans in a grid (`grid-column`). The
value is the span count, e.g. `2` spans 2 columns — NOT the column it
starts at.

gridTemplateColumns.description:
Control number of columns in a grid layout (`grid-template-columns`).
The value is the number of equal columns, e.g. `2` creates two equal
columns (`repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`) — use this instead of a raw
`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

Tabs.description:
Tabs organize content into multiple sections with horizontal navigation.

Use this component when users need to switch between different views of
related content. Don't manually build tab navigation with multiple Buttons in
a Flex - Tabs handles state management, active states, keyboard navigation,
and accessibility automatically.

When not to use:
- For primary/vertical navigation use Sidebar instead.
Tabs.name: Tabs

Whether a tab is activated automatically or manually.
@defaultValue automatic

Text.description:
Renders text content for body copy, descriptions, and general text without semantic meaning. 🚨 Renders a `

` tag by default, which cannot be nested inside another `

` tag. For inline text styling within a paragraph, use `asChild` with a `` element. Use this for paragraphs, captions, descriptions, and non-heading text. When not to use: - For headings and titles use Heading instead. - For form labels use Label instead. - Don't combine large fontSize ('xl'+) with bold fontWeight ('500'+) - that indicates a heading.

h.description: Set the element's height. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

icon.description: Display an icon before or after the button content or omit `children` to only show the icon.

iconPosition.description: Control whether to show the icon before or after the button content.

loading.description: Whether to show loading spinner inside the button.

m.description: Set the element's margin on all sides. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

maxH.description: Set the element's maximum height. Only accepts predefined maxSize tokens.

maxW.description: Set the element's maximum width. Only accepts predefined maxSize tokens.

mb.description: Set the element's bottom margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

ml.description: Set the element's left margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

mr.description: Set the element's right margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

mt.description: Set the element's top margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

mx.description: Set the element's left and right margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

my.description: Set the element's top and bottom margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

onPressedChange.description: The callback that fires when the state of the toggle changes.

p.description: Set the element's padding on all sides. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pb.description: Set the element's bottom padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pl.description: Set the element's left padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pr.description: Set the element's right padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pressed.description: The controlled state of the toggle.

pt.description: Set the element's top padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

px.description: Set the element's left and right padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

py.description: Set the element's top and bottom padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

rounded.description: Set the element's border radius. Only accepts predefined borderRadius tokens.

shadow.description: Set the element's box shadow. Only accepts predefined boxShadow tokens.

size.description: Control the size of the button.

square.description:
Whether button should have square shape (an icon-only button with no
label).

transition.description:
Control which CSS properties should transition. Each value is a preset:
`colors` transitions color-related properties (background, border,
color, fill, stroke, text-decoration); `opacity` and `transform`
transition just that property; `all` transitions everything; `none`
disables transitions.

type.description: The default behavior of the button.

w.description: Set the element's width. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

z.description: Set the element's stacking order. Only accepts predefined zIndex tokens (e.g., popover, toast, tooltip) or numeric values (0, 10, 20, 30, auto).

Tooltip.description:
Popup with brief information shown when user interacts with an element using
keyboard focus or mouse hover.
Tooltip.name: Tooltip

aria-label.description: A more descriptive label for accessibility purpose

auto.description: Enable this option to only show the tooltip when children is partially hidden due to text overflow.

bg.description: Set the element's background color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `bg.` (e.g., `bg.default`, `bg.accent`, `bg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

borderColor.description: Set the element's border color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `border.` (e.g., `border.default`, `border.accent`, `border.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

color.description: Set the element's text color. Only accepts predefined color tokens starting with `fg.` (e.g., `fg.default`, `fg.accent`, `fg.error`), or the special values `current` and `transparent`.

defaultOpen.description: The initial open state in uncontrolled mode.

disabled.description: Whether to show or hide tooltip even if there's content.

flex.description:
Set the element's `flex` CSS property. `1` grows to fill available
space; `auto` grows and shrinks to fit content; `initial` shrinks but
does not grow; `none` neither grows nor shrinks (use this instead of
`flex-shrink: 0`).

fontFamily.description: Set the element's font family. Only accepts predefined fontFamily tokens.

fontSize.description: Set the element's font size and line height (both properties are set together). Only accepts predefined fontSize tokens.

gridAutoRows.description:
Set the element's `grid-auto-rows` CSS property. The only value `fr`
sizes implicit rows to `minmax(0, 1fr)` (equal-height rows that can
shrink below their content).

gridColumn.description:
Set how many columns the element spans in a grid (`grid-column`). The
value is the span count, e.g. `2` spans 2 columns — NOT the column it
starts at.

gridTemplateColumns.description:
Control number of columns in a grid layout (`grid-template-columns`).
The value is the number of equal columns, e.g. `2` creates two equal
columns (`repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`) — use this instead of a raw
`style={{ gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr" }}`.

h.description: Set the element's height. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

m.description: Set the element's margin on all sides. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

maxH.description: Set the element's maximum height. Only accepts predefined maxSize tokens.

maxW.description: Set the element's maximum width. Only accepts predefined maxSize tokens.

mb.description: Set the element's bottom margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

ml.description: Set the element's left margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

mr.description: Set the element's right margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

mt.description: Set the element's top margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

mx.description: Set the element's left and right margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

my.description: Set the element's top and bottom margin. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

onEscapeKeyDown.description:
Event handler called when the escape key is down.
Can be prevented.

onOpenChange.description: Handler that is called when the open state changes.

onPointerDownOutside.description:
Event handler called when the a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `Tooltip`.
Can be prevented.

open.description: The open state in controlled mode.

p.description: Set the element's padding on all sides. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pb.description: Set the element's bottom padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pl.description: Set the element's left padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pr.description: Set the element's right padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

pt.description: Set the element's top padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

px.description: Set the element's left and right padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

py.description: Set the element's top and bottom padding. Only accepts predefined spacing tokens.

rounded.description: Set the element's border radius. Only accepts predefined borderRadius tokens.

shadow.description: Set the element's box shadow. Only accepts predefined boxShadow tokens.

size.description:
Set the element's width and height. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

When width and height are the same, use `size` instead of setting both
`w` and `h` separately (e.g., prefer `size="24"` over `w="24" h="24"`).

transition.description:
Control which CSS properties should transition. Each value is a preset:
`colors` transitions color-related properties (background, border,
color, fill, stroke, text-decoration); `opacity` and `transform`
transition just that property; `all` transitions everything; `none`
disables transitions.

w.description: Set the element's width. Only accepts predefined size tokens.

z.description: Set the element's stacking order. Only accepts predefined zIndex tokens (e.g., popover, toast, tooltip) or numeric values (0, 10, 20, 30, auto).

TooltipProvider.description: Provider component for tooltips. This is included in AxiomProvider by default - you only need to use this component directly for advanced customization.
TooltipProvider.name: TooltipProvider

delayDuration.description:
The duration from when the pointer enters the trigger until the tooltip gets opened.
@defaultValue 700

disableHoverableContent.description: When `true`, trying to hover the content will result in the tooltip closing as the pointer leaves the trigger.

skipDelayDuration.description:
How much time a user has to enter another trigger without incurring a delay again.
@defaultValue 300

Transition.description: Component for animating element transitions with fade and pop effects.
Transition.name: Transition

duration.description: Control the duration of the animation.

skipAnimations.description: Whether to skip the animation or not. Useful for initial mount and unit testing.

type.description:
The properties to animate during transition.
 - fade: only animate the opacity
 - pop: animate the opacity, scale, and translate (depending on data-side)

TransitionGroup.description: Manages the presence and animation lifecycle of children during transitions.
TransitionGroup.name: TransitionGroup

getting-started.content:
# Getting Started

> **Note:** 
  Want to skip the docs? Try our [MCP Server](/guides/mcp/)

## Installation

### Install package

Install the `@optiaxiom/react` package using your package manager of choice:

```sh npm2yarn
npm install @optiaxiom/react
```

### Setup CSS imports

Make sure to configure your bundler to handle [CSS Imports](/guides/css-imports).

### Add global provider

Wrap your application with [AxiomProvider](/components/axiom-provider):

```jsx
import { AxiomProvider } from "@optiaxiom/react";

export function App() {
  return <AxiomProvider>{/* Your app code here */}</AxiomProvider>;
}
```

## Usage

### Basic usage

Import components from `@optiaxiom/react` and use them:

```jsx
import { Button } from "@optiaxiom/react";

export function MyComponent() {
  return <Button>Hello World!</Button>;
}
```

### What to read next

fonts.content:
# Fonts (Optimizely staff only)

We ship our licensed brand fonts as a private package for use with our Optimizely Design System.

> **Note:** 
**Optimizely staff** should always use the fonts from our **`@optimizely/axiom-fonts`** package.

**Third party developers** can skip this package - we automatically fall back to free web fonts.

## Installation

### Install package

Make sure you have access to our private package repository and install the `@optimizely/axiom-fonts` package.

```sh npm2yarn
npm install @optimizely/axiom-fonts
```

### Basic usage

Add a bare import statement at the top of your application's entry point (such as your root layout or main entry file) so the fonts CSS gets loaded.

```tsx
import "@optimizely/axiom-fonts";
```

Make sure the import stays static and synchronous - keep it as a top-level `import` rather than a dynamic `import()` or lazy-loaded module - so the fonts are available from first paint and you avoid a flash of fallback fonts.

## How it works

### Font fallbacks

Our font stacks already list our **licensed brand fonts** first, followed by free web fonts. The free fonts are loaded automatically by the design system, so text always renders.

### Loading the brand fonts

The `@optimizely/axiom-fonts` package adds the `@font-face` declarations for the brand fonts. Once it's imported, the browser uses them at the front of the stack; without it, text gracefully falls back to the free web fonts.
fonts.name: fonts
fonts.title: Fonts

icons.content:
# Icons

We use **Google Material Symbols** icons with our Optimizely Design System via the `@optiaxiom/icons` package.

## Installation

### Install package

```sh npm2yarn
npm install @optiaxiom/icons
```

### Basic usage

Simply import the icon components and use them in your application.

```tsx
import { IconDelete } from "@optiaxiom/icons";

<IconDelete />;
```

Icons accept a `size` prop to control their dimensions:

```tsx
import { IconDelete } from "@optiaxiom/icons";

<IconDelete size="sm" />;
```

Each icon also has a filled variant via the `filled` prop:

```tsx
import { IconDelete } from "@optiaxiom/icons";

<IconDelete filled />;
```

### Available Icons

<IconGallery />

group-migration.content:
# Flex to Group migration ## Why Group? ### Background The [Flex](/components/flex) component has non-standard defaults that cause confusion for developers and AI tools. [Group](/components/group) is the replacement with CSS-standard defaults that align with how flexbox works in standard CSS. ### Key differences | Property | Flex (deprecated) | Group (new) | | ------------- | ----------------- | -------------------- | | flexDirection | "column" | "row" (CSS standard) | | gap | "16" | no default | ## Migration examples ### Vertical grouping (most common old usage) ```tsx // Before
1
2
 // After
1
2
 ``` ### Horizontal layout ```tsx // Before
1
2
 // After
1
2
 ``` Note: Since Group defaults to `flexDirection="row"`, you don't need to specify it explicitly. ### With custom gap ```tsx // Before
1
2
 // After
1
2
 ``` ### Horizontal with custom gap ```tsx // Before
1
2
 // After
1
2
 ``` ### Complex layouts with alignment ```tsx // Before
Left
Right
 // After
Left
Right
 ``` ## Automated migration ### Using the codemod Run the codemod (available in v1.8.0): ```bash npx @optiaxiom/codemod flex-to-group src/ ``` The codemod will: - Transform `` → `` - Transform `` → `` - Transform `` → `` - Update imports: `import { Flex }` → `import { Group }` - Preserve all other props and children ## Manual migration ### Step-by-step checklist If you prefer to migrate manually, follow these steps: 1. **Import Group instead of Flex:** ```tsx // Before import { Flex } from "@optiaxiom/react"; // After import { Group } from "@optiaxiom/react"; ``` 2. **Update component usage:** - If you had no `flexDirection` prop: add `flexDirection="column" gap="16"` - If you had `flexDirection="row"`: remove it (that's the Group default) - If you had `flexDirection="column"`: keep it - If you had no `gap` prop: add `gap="16"` (or your desired gap) 3. **Test your changes:** - Verify layouts render correctly - Check responsive breakpoints still work - Ensure alignments are as expected ## Common patterns ### Form layouts ```tsx // Before // After ``` ### Button groups ```tsx // Before // After ``` ### Card content ```tsx // Before Title Description // After Title Description ``` ## Timeline ### Release schedule - **v1.8.0 (Current):** Group introduced, Flex deprecated, codemod available - **v2.0.0 (Future):** Flex removed, Group renamed to Flex with new defaults

responsive-styles.content: # Responsive Styles `Box` component support responsive styles for all layout related props. ## Documentation ### Object notation Use the object prop notation where the key is the breakpoint name. ```tsx import { Group } from "@optiaxiom/react"; export function App() { return ...; } ``` ### Array notation Use the array prop notation with the elements in order of condition `[base, sm, md]`. ```tsx import { Group } from "@optiaxiom/react"; export function App() { return ...; } ``` ### Example ```tsx import { Box, Group, Text } from "@optiaxiom/react"; export function App() { return ( Phasellus auctor Nullam rhoncus gravida urna Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet conse ctetur adipiscing elit. Phasellus efficitur feugiat luctus et. ); } ```
